findings.notes: Turso/libSQL lacks .pragma() method and PRAGMA result-set semantics differ; schema introspection must use libSQL-specific API or INFORMATION_SCHEMA
better-sqlite3 synchronous transaction wrapper; libSQL client is async-first and transaction API differs fundamentally
better-sqlite3 .run() returns { changes, lastInsertRowid }; libSQL async execute() returns different result shape requiring adapter
better-sqlite3 .exec() runs multi-statement DDL synchronously; libSQL .execute() is async and multi-statement batching differs

findings.signature: CREATE TRIGGER IF NOT EXISTS prevent_ledger_update BEFORE UPDATE ON mutation_ledger BEGIN SELECT RAISE(ABORT, '...'); END
findings.notes: Trigger DDL syntax with BEGIN/END blocks; Turso/libSQL trigger support may differ in RAISE() semantics and BEFORE triggers
JSON function in index expression is SQLite 3.38+; Turso/libSQL JSON support may not cover expression indexes on json_extract
Table-valued function json_each() for array expansion; supported in libSQL but semantics may diverge for nested JSON

findings.signature: SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type = 'trigger' AND tbl_name = 'mutation_ledger'
findings.notes: Direct sqlite_master query for trigger verification; Turso may expose schema via different system tables or API
strftime with millisecond format %f in DEFAULT expression; libSQL may not support %f in default value expressions
better-sqlite3 result property lastInsertRowid; libSQL async execute returns different result structure needing adapter
json_extract in WHERE for filtering; libSQL supports JSON functions but performance characteristics may differ for large tables

findings.signature: INSERT ... ON CONFLICT(receipt_key) DO UPDATE SET ...
findings.notes: Upsert with ON CONFLICT; supported in libSQL but verify DO UPDATE SET excluded.column syntax matches exactly
datetime('now') built-in; supported in libSQL but verify timezone handling consistency
Dynamic IN clause with parameterized placeholders; standard SQL, supported in libSQL but verify parameter binding API

findings.signature: database.prepare(`SELECT ... WHERE relation IN ('contradicts','supersedes') AND source_id IN (${placeholders})`)
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 synchronous .prepare().all(); libSQL async execute() requires await and different parameter passing convention

findings.signature: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3'; let db: Database.Database | null
findings.notes: better-sqlite3 type namespace used throughout; migration requires replacing with libSQL client types and async wrappers
